METHOD SECTION.problem_domain: Numerical integration of large-scale stiff systems of ODEs, which arise from the spatial discretization of PDEs using the method of lines.
METHOD SECTION.standard_approach: Implicit methods are preferred for stiff systems. A common implementation is the Jacobian-Free Newton-Krylov algorithm, which uses a Newton iteration and a Krylov subspace solver for the linear systems.
METHOD SECTION.limitation_of_standard_approach: The main challenge with implicit methods is constructing an effective preconditioner for the Krylov solver, which is often difficult and a subject of research.
METHOD SECTION.proposed_alternative: Exponential integrators are presented as an efficient alternative. They have good stability properties allowing for large timesteps and can offer computational savings per timestep compared to implicit integrators, especially when a fast preconditioner is not available.
METHOD SECTION.core_computational_task: The dominant cost of exponential integrators is the computation of exponential-like φ-functions applied to a vector.
METHOD SECTION.standard_technique_for_task: Krylov subspace methods are used to efficiently compute these matrix functions by projecting the matrix onto a low-dimensional subspace.
METHOD SECTION.specific_algorithm_and_its_limitation: For non-symmetric matrices, the Arnoldi iteration is the standard Krylov method. However, its parallel scalability is poor due to the global communication required for inner products in the Gram-Schmidt orthogonalization process, which becomes a bottleneck.

RESULTS SECTION.status: The provided text for the RESULTS SECTION is truncated. No specific results, performance metrics, or comparative analyses could be extracted from the given content.

title: Low-synchronization Arnoldi Methods for the Matrix Exponential with Application to Exponential Integrators
